Apple fixes bug that let the FBI recover deleted Signal messages
items.summary: Apple has released out-of-band security updates for iPhone and iPad devices to fix a Notification Services flaw that could allow notifications marked for deletion to remain stored on the device. [...]

items.title: 'The Gentlemen' Rapidly Rises to Ransomware Prominence
items.summary: Not nearly as polite as the name suggests, the ransomware gang has impressed researchers with its speed in scaling up operations — and its sophistication.

items.title: New Mirai campaign exploits RCE flaw in EoL D-Link routers
items.summary: A new Mirai-based malware campaign is actively exploiting CVE-2025-29635, a high-severity command-injection vulnerability affecting D-Link DIR-823X routers, to enlist devices into the botnet. [...]

items.title: Kyber ransomware gang toys with post-quantum encryption on Windows
items.summary: A new Kyber ransomware operation is targeting Windows systems and VMware ESXi endpoints in recent attacks, with one variant implementing Kyber1024 post-quantum encryption. [...]

items.title: Malicious KICS Docker Images and VS Code Extensions Hit Checkmarx Supply Chain
items.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of malicious images pushed to the official "checkmarx/kics" Docker Hub repository. In an alert published today, software supply chain security company Socket revealed that unknown threat actors…

items.title: Self-Propagating Supply Chain Worm Hijacks npm Packages to Steal Developer Tokens
items.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh set of packages that have been compromised by bad actors to deliver a self-propagating worm that spreads through stolen developer npm tokens. The supply chain worm has been detected by both…

items.title: Harvester Deploys Linux GoGra Backdoor in South Asia Using Microsoft Graph API
items.summary: The threat actor known as Harvester has been attributed to a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor deployed as part of attacks likely targeting entities in South Asia. "The malware uses the legitimate Microsoft Graph API and Outlook…

items.title: Spain dismantles major $4.7M manga piracy platform, arrests four
items.summary: The Spanish police have dismantled the largest Spanish-language manga piracy platform, operating since 2014, with millions of monthly users from around the globe. [...]

items.title: DPRK Fake Job Scams Self-Propagate in 'Contagious Interview'
items.summary: A compromised developer's repository serves as a worm-like infection vector to spread remote access Trojans (RATs) and other malware.

items.title: Inside Caller-as-a-Service Fraud: The Scam Economy Has a Hiring Process
items.summary: Fraud operations now operate like call centers, complete with hiring, training, and performance tracking. Flare reveals how cybercriminals manage "Caller-as-a-Service" operations like a professional sales team. [...]

items.title: New npm supply-chain attack self-spreads to steal auth tokens
items.summary: A new supply chain attack targeting the Node Package Manager (npm) ecosystem is stealing developer credentials and attempting to spread through packages published from compromised accounts. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Teams to get efficiency mode on PCs with limited resources
items.summary: Microsoft is preparing to roll out a new Efficiency Mode for Microsoft Teams for systems with limited CPU and memory resources to improve app responsiveness. [...]

items.title: Lotus Wiper Malware Targets Venezuelan Energy Systems in Destructive Attack
items.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented data wiper that has been used in attacks targeting Venezuela at the end of last year and the start of 2026. Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the novel file wiper has been used in a…

items.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
items.summary: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents. The more…

items.title: Microsoft traces Universal Print issues to Graph API code change
items.summary: Microsoft says that an ongoing Universal Print sharing issue that prevents users from creating some printer shares is due to a Microsoft Graph API code change. [...]

items.title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
items.summary: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372, carries a CVSS score of 9.1 out of 10.0.…

items.title: Mustang Panda’s New LOTUSLITE Variant Targets India Banks, South Korea Policy Circles
items.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new variant of a known malware called LOTUSLITE that's distributed via a theme related to India's banking sector. "The backdoor communicates with a dynamic DNS-based command-and-control server…

items.summary: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5752, is rated 9.3 on the CVSS scoring system. "Sandbox…

items.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to BlackCat Scheme
items.summary: A cautionary tale illustrates why the person negotiating should never be involved with any part of the ransom payment process.

items.title: Exploits Turn Windows Defender Into Attacker Tool
items.summary: Three proof-of-concept exploits are being used in active attacks against Microsoft's built-in security platform; two are unpatched.

items.title: Surge in Bomgar RMM Exploitation Demonstrates Supply Chain Risk
items.summary: The critical remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-1731) in the remote monitoring and management tool can be exploited to spread ransomware and compromise supply chains.

items.title: Google Fixes Critical RCE Flaw in AI-Based 'Antigravity' Tool
items.summary: The prompt-injection vulnerability in the agentic AI product for filesystem operations was a sanitization issue that allowed for sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution.

items.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.summary: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message…

items.title: Chinese APT Targets Indian Banks, Korean Policy Circles
items.summary: China is spying on India's financial sector, for some reason, and it's not putting much effort into it, judging by some stale TTPs.

items.title: A .WAV With A Payload, (Tue, Apr 21st)
items.summary: There have been reports of threat actors using a .wav file as a vector for malware.

Multiples vulnérabilités dans Drupal (16 avril 2026)
items.summary: De multiples vulnérabilités ont été découvertes dans Drupal. Elles permettent à un attaquant de provoquer une exécution de code arbitraire à distance, une injection SQL (SQLi) et une injection de code indirecte à distance (XSS).

items.title: Multiples vulnérabilités dans Google Chrome (16 avril 2026)
items.summary: De multiples vulnérabilités ont été découvertes dans Google Chrome. Elles permettent à un attaquant de provoquer un problème de sécurité non spécifié par l'éditeur.

items.title: Vulnérabilité dans Apache Kafka (16 avril 2026)
items.summary: Une vulnérabilité a été découverte dans Apache Kafka. Elle permet à un attaquant de provoquer une atteinte à la confidentialité des données et une atteinte à l'intégrité des données.

items.summary: De multiples vulnérabilités ont été découvertes dans Microsoft Azure. Elles permettent à un attaquant de provoquer une élévation de privilèges.
